user_query:
I want a small, reproducible system status inspection for my Android dev workstation that also helps me phase out toasts in a sample file. Please do the following in this repo:

1) Implement a Python 3 script at tools/inspect_android_env.py that reads a YAML config at config/checks.yaml (you create it) to determine thresholds and patterns, then writes a JSON report to out/system_status.json. The script must perform these checks and produce exactly this JSON shape:
{
  "os": {"uname": string},
  "java": {"present": bool, "version": string|null},
  "android_sdk": {"env_var": string|null, "path": string|null, "platforms_exists": bool|null, "build_tools_exists": bool|null, "min_build_tools_satisfied": bool|null},
  "adb": {"present": bool, "server_running": bool|null, "connected_devices": integer},
  "disk": {"free_gb": number, "disk_ok": bool},
  "gradle_cache": {"path": string|null, "size_mb": number|null},
  "log_summary": {"toast_lines": integer, "error_lines": integer, "pattern_counts": object},
  "build_log": {"status": "SUCCESS"|"FAILED"|"UNKNOWN", "duration": string|null}
}
Required behavior for the script:
- Read thresholds and patterns from config/checks.yaml with keys: min_disk_gb (number), min_build_tools_versions (list of string prefixes), log_patterns (list of strings). Use these to set disk.disk_ok and android_sdk.min_build_tools_satisfied, and to count occurrences in logs.
- Collect OS info via uname -a. Detect Java via "java -version". Detect Android SDK using ANDROID_SDK_ROOT or ANDROID_HOME env vars; if set, check existence of platforms/ and build-tools/ subdirs, and whether any build-tools subdir matches one of min_build_tools_versions prefixes. Detect adb by looking up in PATH; if present, try starting the server (adb start-server) and count connected devices via "adb devices" (if adb missing, set present:false and other fields null/0 accordingly). Compute free disk space for the project directory’s filesystem in GB. If ~/.gradle/caches exists, compute its size in MB.
- Parse provided logs/logcat_sample.txt to count lines containing the literal substring "Toast" (case-sensitive) and count lines that start with an Android log priority of "E/" (error lines). Also count each string specified in log_patterns and return them in log_summary.pattern_counts as a map from pattern to integer.
- Parse provided logs/gradle_build.log to extract the last build status: set build_log.status to SUCCESS if it contains a line "BUILD SUCCESSFUL", FAILED if it contains "BUILD FAILED", otherwise UNKNOWN. If a duration like "in 1m 12s" appears on the BUILD line, capture that as build_log.duration; otherwise null.
- Write the JSON to out/system_status.json and print a single line to stdout: "Status report written to out/system_status.json". The script must exit with code 0 even if some tools are missing; mark missing tools with present:false and fill other fields with null or safe defaults as described.

2) Create config/checks.yaml with reasonable defaults so the script can run without edits. Minimum required keys and example values:
- min_disk_gb: 5
- min_build_tools_versions: ["34.", "33."]
- log_patterns: ["Network", "Crashlytics"]
The script must read and use these values.

3) Modify app/src/main/java/com/example/StatusNotifier.kt to remove all Toast usage and replace it with android.util.Log.d using the same message text. Keep function signatures unchanged and add a comment line exactly: "Replaced Toast with Log to avoid UI popups." Do not introduce external dependencies.

4) Run tools/inspect_android_env.py to generate out/system_status.json.

5) Draft a concise Slack-ready status message (4–6 sentences) summarizing: disk.disk_ok, java.version (or "missing"), adb.present and adb.connected_devices, build_log.status and duration (if present), and the counts of toast_lines and error_lines from log_summary. Also mention the Toast removal in StatusNotifier.kt and include the relative path to out/system_status.json. Save this as out/slack_update.txt.

Inputs you must use:
- logs/logcat_sample.txt (parse for toast/error lines and patterns)
- logs/gradle_build.log (parse for build status)
- app/src/main/java/com/example/StatusNotifier.kt (modify to remove Toast)

Deliverables (must exist and be populated at these exact paths):
- config/checks.yaml (created by you)
- tools/inspect_android_env.py (executable with Python 3)
- out/system_status.json (generated by running the script)
- app/src/main/java/com/example/StatusNotifier.kt (modified per above)
- out/slack_update.txt (the drafted message)

Constraints:
- Use only standard tools on Linux/macOS (bash/sh, Python 3, coreutils). No Internet access required.
- Do not delete any files. Create directories as needed under the workspace.
- The JSON must be syntactically valid and use the exact key names above.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
